metadata.summary: Two-stage pipeline: Haiku transcribes interviews; Sonnet extracts themes per interview; Opus synthesizes cross-interview patterns at scale.

content.title: AI-Assisted Interview Analysis
content.summary: A two-stage pipeline for transcribing recorded user interviews and extracting themes, sentiment, and cross-interview patterns at scale. Haiku handles transcription; Sonnet extracts themes per interview; Opus synthesizes patterns across interviews. Human review is required between stages 2 and 3.

why.p: Manual transcription and thematic analysis of 10+ interviews takes days; AI reduces it to hours without removing researcher judgment from the critical step (cross-interview synthesis). Accuracy benchmarks: up to 99% transcription accuracy on good audio, ~80-85% sentiment accuracy — the latter always requires human review.

list.item: Transcribing recorded user interviews or usability sessions (audio/video files).
Extracting themes and sentiment across a batch of 5+ interviews in parallel.
Building a research repository searchable by insight, theme, or participant.
Pre-processing transcripts before human thematic analysis to remove mechanical work.
When the interview moderation itself needs to be automated — AI cannot facilitate nuanced probing
When the research question requires nonverbal behavioral data (gaze, hesitation, body language)
When audio quality is poor (heavy accent + background noise) — transcription accuracy drops below 80%, making analysis unreliable
When participant confidentiality is high-risk and SaaS tools cannot be used — run local transcription (Whisper) instead
As a replacement for the researcher's interpretive judgment — themes require human validation before becoming findings

section.name: tool-landscape
section.p: Transcription and analysis tool comparison, accuracy benchmarks, time savings, and structural limitations of AI-assisted interview analysis.

list.item: Looppanel — UX research focus; 17 languages; up to 90%+ accuracy; insight tagging; API beta
Dovetail — research repository + highlights API; best for structured knowledge base; API available
Insight7 — bulk interview analysis; theme extraction API; strong for 20+ interview batches
UserBit — 50+ languages; speaker identification; limited API exposure
AssemblyAI — transcription + sentiment + entity recognition API; most developer-friendly
Otter.ai — real-time transcription API; best for live meeting capture
Fireflies.ai — meeting transcription + CRM integration; strongest for sales/CS interviews
Whisper (OpenAI OSS) — local transcription; no data leaves machine; pip install openai-whisper
Descript — video editing + transcription; no programmatic access to transcript data
Transcription: up to 99% accuracy on good audio (quiet room, clear speech)
Speaker diarization: good reliability with 2 speakers; degrades with 3+ or overlapping speech
Sentiment analysis: approximately 80-85%; irony, sarcasm, and cultural nuance consistently misclassified

rule.statement: Pre-process audio with ffmpeg to normalize volume and reduce background noise before transcription; 5 dB SNR improvement significantly increases accuracy.
rule.rationale: Transcription models are sensitive to signal-to-noise ratio. Post-processing a bad transcript is more expensive than pre-processing the audio.

list.item: Transcription: hours → minutes
Summarization: hours → seconds
Theme extraction: days → hours
Cross-interview patterns: weeks → hours

rule.statement: Always send a speaker diarization prompt before theme extraction; mixed-speaker text produces unreliable sentiment and attribution errors.
rule.rationale: Speaker attribution errors in transcription propagate into theme analysis. Themes attributed to the wrong participant distort the entire analysis.

list.item: Cannot observe or facilitate moderated studies
Cannot understand nonverbal interactions (gaze, hesitation, body language)
Text-focused; limited behavioral analysis
Research tools lock analysis inside proprietary repositories; exporting structured insights for downstream agents requires API access
AI summaries sometimes omit low-frequency but high-salience quotes; human review of the full transcript remains essential

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Three-stage Haiku/Sonnet/Opus pipeline for interview transcription, theme extraction, and cross-interview synthesis with mandatory human review gate.

rule.statement: Analyze interviews independently first (per-interview themes), then synthesize across them; never feed all transcripts to one agent call.
rule.rationale: Context window limits distort results when large transcript batches are processed together. Independent analysis followed by synthesis preserves per-interview signal.

rule.statement: Require the agent to cite a verbatim quote for every theme it identifies; reject themes without transcript evidence.
rule.rationale: LLMs will hallucinate themes that sound plausible but do not appear in the source transcript. Quote citation makes hallucination detectable.

rule.statement: Tag themes with participant count (e.g., "3/8 participants mentioned X") so stakeholders can assess confidence without reading raw transcripts.
rule.rationale: Frequency data is the primary signal for distinguishing genuine patterns from outlier observations. Without it, all themes appear equally significant.

rule.statement: Require human review between theme extraction (Sonnet) and cross-interview synthesis (Opus); do not automate this handoff.
rule.rationale: Agents cannot distinguish between a genuinely surprising insight and a transcription artifact. A researcher must validate theme labels before Opus synthesizes patterns across them.

rule.statement: Never omit minority views; low-frequency but emotionally intense quotes often contain the most actionable insights.
rule.rationale: Frequency-only weighting silences edge cases. A single participant quote expressing extreme frustration can identify a blocking issue that the majority simply tolerates.

rule.statement: Chunk long transcripts (60+ minutes) by topic or time segment and merge summaries; do not process dense transcripts in a single call.
rule.rationale: Agents lose nuance when given dense long transcripts in one call; early sections get more attention than later ones due to positional bias.

rule.statement: Weight behavioral observations over stated sentiment in formal interview contexts; sentiment analysis on research interviews is less useful than on consumer feedback.
rule.rationale: Interview context is formal and positive bias is common; participants understate frustrations in directed sessions. Behavioral signals (what they did) are more reliable than emotional labels.

list.item: Speaker diarization degrades with 3+ speakers or overlapping speech
Sentiment analysis accuracy is approximately 80-85%; irony, sarcasm, and cultural nuance are consistently misclassified
Topic modeling conflates distinct themes when transcripts are short (less than 10 minutes per session)
Insight extraction is text-only; behavioral observations from screen shares or physical usability tests are invisible to the model
Research tools lock analysis inside proprietary repositories; exporting structured insights for downstream agents requires manual work or API access
AI-generated summaries sometimes omit low-frequency but high-salience quotes — human review of the full transcript remains essential for key findings

template.purpose: Whisper batch transcription: folder of audio files to JSON transcripts

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Best practices for AI-assisted interview analysis:

list.item: Pre-process audio with ffmpeg to normalize volume and reduce background noise before transcription — 5 dB improvement in SNR significantly increases accuracy
Always send a speaker diarization prompt ("Identify each speaker as Speaker 1, Speaker 2...") before theme extraction — mixed-speaker text produces unreliable sentiment
Use Dovetail or Looppanel as the canonical research repository; run agents against exported transcripts, not against the SaaS UI
For cross-interview synthesis, analyze interviews independently first, then synthesize — do not feed all transcripts to one agent call; context window limits distort results
Tag themes with participant count ("3/8 participants mentioned X") so stakeholders can assess confidence without reading raw transcripts
Never omit minority views; low-frequency but emotionally intense quotes often contain the most actionable insights

section.name: ai-agent-pitfalls
section.p: Critical pitfalls when agents perform interview analysis:

list.item: Agents lose nuance when given dense 60+ minute transcripts in a single call; chunk by topic or time segment and merge summaries

Sentiment on research interviews is systematically less useful than in consumer feedback — interview context is formal and positive bias is common; weight behavioral observations over stated sentiment
LLMs will hallucinate themes that sound plausible but do not appear in the source transcript; require the agent to cite a verbatim quote for every theme it identifies
Speaker attribution errors in transcription propagate into theme analysis; human spot-check of speaker labels is necessary before synthesis
Human checkpoint required before finalizing findings: agents cannot distinguish between a genuinely surprising insight and a transcription artifact
